content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Review Agent — Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis
2	
3	You are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) maintain the blueprint markers that require *semantic* judgement (`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations), and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	`\leanok` placement is handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs immediately before you. **Do not add or remove `\leanok` yourself.** If a `\leanok` you expect is missing, the underlying Lean source still has a sorry or doesn't compile — investigate, don't paper over.
6	
7	**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs.** You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts as part of your analysis. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint-marker files.
8	
9	## Iteration number — canonical
10	
11	Your invocation prompt contains `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. As of 2026 these are the **same number** — `session_M/` is always the review of `iter-NNN` (i.e. M == NNN). Use the iteration form (`iter-NNN`) when referencing the iteration in prose (`summary.md` metadata, recommendations titles like "Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-{NNN+1:03d})") and the bare integer (`session_M`) when referring to the review-output directory. If older `summary.md` / `recommendations.md` files use the legacy independent session counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents — your new file uses the iteration number.
12	
13	## Step 1: Identify Context
14	
15	1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` — count existing session folders to determine the current session number.
16	2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.
17	3. Run `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` to see what changed.
18	
19	## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)
20	
21	**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.
22	
23	The file contains:
24	
25	- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)
26	- Remaining lines: One event per tool call — edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds
27	
28	**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text → new_text).
29	**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.
30	**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.
31	**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.
32	
33	This is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.
34	
35	## Step 3: Read Recent History
36	
37	If previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
38	
39	## Step 4: Write Proof Journal
40	
41	Create the session folder and write two files:
42	
43	```bash
44	mkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>
45	```
46	
47	### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`
48	
49	Must include:
50	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)
51	- For EACH target attempted:
52	  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point
53	  - What was learned from each failed attempt
54	  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas
55	- Key findings / proof patterns discovered
56	- Recommendations for next session
57	
58	### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`
59	
60	Each line MUST follow this JSON format — one entry per target theorem:
61	
62	```json
63	{
64	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
65	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
66	  "target": {
67	    "file": "path/to/File.lean",
68	    "theorem": "theorem_name"
69	  },
70	  "session": {
71	    "id": "session_N",
72	    "model": "model-name"
73	  },
74	  "findings": {
75	    "blocker": "description if blocked",
76	    "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1", "lemma2"]
77	  },
78	  "attempts": [
79	    {
80	      "attempt": 1,
81	      "strategy": "what was tried",
82	      "code_tried": "actual Lean code or tactic",
83	      "line_number": "line number in the initial file where this code was attempted",
84	      "lean_error": "actual error message if failed",
85	      "goal_before": "the goal state before this attempt",
86	      "goal_after": "the goal state after this attempt",
87	      "result": "success|failed|partial",
88	      "insight": "what was learned from this attempt"
89	    }
90	  ],
91	  "next_steps": "..."
92	}
93	```
94	
95	**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:
96	- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded
97	- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)
98	- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one
99	
100	### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`
101	
102	Write concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:
103	- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized
104	- Which approaches showed promise but need more work
105	- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)
106	- Any reusable proof patterns discovered
107	
108	If your analysis shows the prover has hit the exact same blocker for several consecutive iterations on the same target, you should explicitly instruct the Plan Agent to avoid retrying the same approach without putting more effort into understanding the underlying issue.
109	
110	## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md (Knowledge Base only)
111	
112	The per-session narrative (Overall Progress: total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, this session's analysis) goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` — see the next subsection. PROJECT_STATUS.md carries only the cumulative Knowledge Base:
113	
114	```markdown
115	# Project Status
116	
117	## Knowledge Base
118	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
119	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
120	
121	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
122	- <target>: <reason>
123	
124	## Last Updated
125	<ISO timestamp>
126	```
127	
128	If an existing PROJECT_STATUS.md still carries an "Overall Progress" section from a prior project that grew it, leave the old content where it is (do not delete history) but stop appending to it. New session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
129	
130	### Per-iteration sidecars (split write)
131	
132	Your invocation prompt contains a `## Per-iteration sidecars` block that names `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` as the destination for THIS session's narrative. Split your writes accordingly:
133	
134	- **The per-session "Overall Progress" narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**, NOT to PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Overall Progress" section. PROJECT_STATUS.md no longer carries a session log.
135	- **You DO keep updating PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Knowledge Base" section.** Cumulative non-obvious facts (errors not to reproduce, reusable proof patterns, Mathlib idioms that worked) still belong in the Knowledge Base. The Knowledge Base is the only growing-but-curated part of PROJECT_STATUS.md.
136	- **Format for `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**: same fields as the Overall Progress block above (Total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, plus any narrative analysis). Born-bounded — this file contains THIS session only, never a multi-session log.
137	- Older `iter/iter-MMM/review.md` files are on disk for you to read on demand, but the recent window is already injected in your prompt.
138	
139	## Step 6: Blueprint Markers
140	
141	`\leanok` placement is now handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs between the prover and you. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration in the Lean source, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. **Do not touch `\leanok` markers yourself.** If you see one missing where you expect it, the underlying file probably doesn't compile or still has a sorry — do not paper over that with a manual edit.
142	
143	Your remaining marker responsibilities are the ones that require semantic judgement:
144	
145	- **`\mathlibok`** (statement-block only) — declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias / direct reference). The deterministic script never adds or removes this; you decide based on the prover's task result.
146	- **`\lean{...}` macro maintenance** — if a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `\lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.
147	- **`% NOTE: <reason>` annotations** — when a block is unformalized because the informal statement did not translate cleanly, add a `% NOTE: ...` comment explaining the obstacle so the plan agent sees it.
148	- **Stripping `\notready`** — if a `\notready` marker still sits on a block that the prover has now landed, remove it. The deterministic script does not manage `\notready`.
149	
150	### `\mathlibok` rules (your domain)
151	
152	Add `\mathlibok` inside the statement block when:
153	- The Lean side references a Mathlib name directly (`def foo := Mathlib.bar`, `theorem foo := Mathlib.bar`, or a simple `export Mathlib.Foo (bar)`), AND
154	- The Archon-side declaration itself contains no `sorry` and introduces no new proof obligation.
155	
156	If you add `\mathlibok`, no `\leanok` is needed on the proof block — the deterministic script will leave the proof block alone if there's no Lean proof body to verify.
157	
158	### Record what you changed
159	
160	In `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated" section listing **only the changes you personally made** (the deterministic script's `\leanok` adds/removes are committed separately as `archon[NNN/marker-sync]` and don't need to appear here):
161	
162	```markdown
163	## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
164	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
165	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: added `% NOTE: prover reported translation gap, see task_results/Core.md`
166	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
167	- `Core.tex`, `thm:foo`: corrected `\lean{Old.foo}` → `\lean{New.foo}` after refactor rename
168	```
169	
170	### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`
171	
172	While the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. 
173	
174	**Rules:**
175	- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).
176	- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.
177	
178	## Step 7: Dispatch review subagents
179	
180	You may dispatch read-only review subagents BEFORE writing your consolidated summary. They are audits whose findings you incorporate into `summary.md` / `recommendations.md`. Each runs as its own fresh-context Claude process; spawning two or more in one assistant message runs them in parallel, subject to the global `max_parallel` cap.
181	
182	### Where the catalog comes from
183	
184	Your invocation prompt contains an auto-generated **Available subagents** section listing every enabled descriptor. Reviewers are typically marked `[read-only]`. A descriptor tagged `[MANDATORY]` for this phase MUST be dispatched before the phase completes. Do not `ls .archon/subagents/` — the catalog you were handed is authoritative.
185	
186	When you decide to invoke a specific reviewer, read its full prompt and directive shape from `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
187	
188	### How to dispatch
189	
190	Pattern (Bash tool, parallel-able, run **in foreground** so the report lands before you write your summary):
191	
192	```
193	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
194	  --name <subagent-name> \
195	  --slug <kebab-case-slug> \
196	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md \
197	  --write-domain 'task_results/**'
198	```
199	
200	Each directive must be **fully self-contained** — the reviewer reads only what the directive points it at. The directive shape for each reviewer is documented in its descriptor under `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
201	
202	### When NOT to dispatch
203	
204	- If this session was a pure proof-filling round with no new definitions or refactors, skip reviewers. They add latency and cost for no value.
205	- If a previous session in the last 3 iters already ran the same audit on the same scope, don't repeat unless that scope changed.
206	- The plan agent may also have dispatched reviewers proactively — check `task_results/` for matching reports before doubling up.
207	
208	### Incorporating findings
209	
210	After the reviewers return, read every report. Land each finding in your `summary.md` and `recommendations.md`:
211	
212	- **CRITICAL / HIGH** findings → bullet at the top of `recommendations.md` with the suggested action.
213	- **MEDIUM** findings → bullet in `recommendations.md`'s body.
214	- **LOW** findings → one-liner in `summary.md`'s notes section.
215	
216	Do NOT repeat the full report content in your summary — link to the report file. The plan agent reads your summary, not the raw reports.
217	
218	## Step 8: Self-Validation
219	
220	After writing all files, validate your output by checking:
221	- [ ] milestones.jsonl has valid JSON on every line
222	- [ ] Each milestone has `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`
223	- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has at least 1 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`
224	- [ ] Number of attempts per milestone is proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`
225	- [ ] summary.md includes specific code/errors, not just high-level summaries
226	- [ ] recommendations.md includes actionable next steps
227	- [ ] You did NOT add or remove any `\leanok` marker yourself (those are handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you)
228	- [ ] For every Mathlib-backed declaration the prover reported, the blueprint chapter has `\mathlibok` (your domain)
229	- [ ] Any `\lean{...}` macro rename flagged in a `task_results/<file>.md` has been applied
230	- [ ] No `\notready` marker remains on a block whose Lean declaration now exists
231	
232	## Permissions
233	
234	You may write to:
235	- `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
236	- `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`
237	- `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` — markers (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`), `\lean{...}` macro corrections, stale-marker cleanup, and `% NOTE:` comments. Do NOT rewrite the informal prose — that is the plan agent's surface.
238	
239	You must NOT write to:
240	- Any `.lean` files
241	- `.archon/PROGRESS.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
242	- `.archon/task_pending.md` or `.archon/task_done.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
243	- Blueprint informal content (theorem/proof prose, chapter structure) — only the markers and `% NOTE:` comments are yours.

25	
26	# Lean Auditor
27	
28	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
29	
30	You are **mandatory in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you every iteration. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
31	
32	## Stance: no strategic bias
33	
34	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
35	
36	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
37	
38	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
39	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
40	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
41	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
42	
43	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
44	
45	## Directive Format
46	
47	```markdown
48	# Lean Auditor Directive
49	
50	## Slug
51	<slug>
52	
53	## Scope (files)
54	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
55	
56	## Focus areas (optional)
57	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
58	
59	## Known issues
60	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
61	```
62	
63	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
64	
65	## What you do
66	
67	1. Read your directive.
68	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
69	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
70	   - Read the whole file.
71	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
72	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
73	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
74	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
75	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
76	
77	You may use:
78	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
79	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
80	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
81	
82	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
83	
84	## Report format
85	
86	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
87	
88	```markdown
89	# Lean Audit Report
90	
91	## Slug
92	<slug>
93	
94	## Iteration
95	<NNN>
96	
97	## Scope
98	- files audited: <N>
99	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
100	
101	## Per-file checklist
102	
103	### <path/to/file.lean>
104	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
105	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
106	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
107	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
108	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
109	- **notes**:
110	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
111	  - ...
112	
113	### <next file>
114	- ...
115	
116	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
117	
118	## Must-fix-this-iter
119	
120	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
121	
122	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
123	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
124	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
125	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
126	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
127	
128	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
129	
130	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
131	- ...
132	
133	## Major
134	
135	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
136	
137	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
138	- ...
139	
140	## Minor
141	
142	Low-impact observations.
143	
144	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
145	- ...
146	
147	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
148	
149	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
150	
151	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
152	- ...
153	
154	## Severity summary
155	
156	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
157	- **major**: <N>
158	- **minor**: <N>
159	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
160	
161	Overall verdict: one sentence.
162	```
163	
164	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
165	
166	## Return value
167	
168	Your final assistant message:
169	
170	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
171	- The path to your full report.
172	
173	## Reminders
174	
175	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
176	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
177	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
178	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
179	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
180

2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: true
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am mandatory in the review phase. Dispatch one checker per .lean
11	    file that received prover work this iteration — every file, no
12	    exceptions. Skipping any prover-touched file is a review-phase
13	    failure, not editorial discretion. Each dispatch is independent
14	    and parallel-able.
15	  - Also dispatch on any file the lean-auditor flagged as suspicious,
16	    even if it received no prover work this iter.
17	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
18	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
19	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
20	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
21	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide formalization,
22	    missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly needed), not
23	    only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate, the plan agent
24	    should dispatch a blueprint-writer to address it.
25	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
26	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
27	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
28	---
29	
30	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
31	
32	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
33	
34	## Your Job
35	
36	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
37	
38	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
39	
40	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
41	
42	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
43	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
44	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
45	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
46	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
47	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
48	
49	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
50	
51	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
52	
53	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
54	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
55	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
56	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
57	
58	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
59	
60	## Directive Format
61	
62	```markdown
63	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
64	
65	## Slug
66	<slug>
67	
68	## Lean file
69	<path/to/file.lean>
70	
71	## Blueprint chapter
72	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
73	
74	## Known issues
75	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
76	```
77	
78	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the lean-auditor's and blueprint-reviewer's job; you stay focused on this one pair.
79	
80	## What you do

560	    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
561	    HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]
562	  cat_disch

567	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
568	noncomputable def HModule'_sequenceIso
569	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
570	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
571	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
572	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
573	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
574	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
575	    HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h ≅
576	      Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence (HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S)
577	        F n₀ n₁ (by omega) :=
578	  ComposableArrows.isoMk₅ (Iso.refl _)
579	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
580	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
581	    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)
582	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
583	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
584	    (Iso.refl _)
585	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
586	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
587	    (by dsimp; rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]; rfl)
588	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
589	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
590	
591	/-- Iter-026: Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness theorem (mirror Mathlib
592	`MayerVietoris.lean` L140–141). The iter-022 sequence is exact via the iter-023
593	comparison iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence`. -/
594	lemma HModule'_sequence_exact
595	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
596	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
597	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
598	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
599	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
600	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
601	    (HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
602	  ComposableArrows.exact_of_iso (HModule'_sequenceIso k S F n₀ n₁ h).symm
603	    (Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)
604	
605	/-- Iter-026: `δ ≫ toBiprod = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L143–145). -/
606	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
607	lemma HModule'_δ_toBiprod
608	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
609	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
610	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
611	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
612	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
613	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
614	    HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h ≫ HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁ = 0 :=
615	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 2
616	
617	/-- Iter-026: `fromBiprod ≫ δ = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L147–149). -/
618	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
619	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_δ
620	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
621	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
622	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
623	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
624	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
625	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
626	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀ ≫ HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h = 0 :=
627	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 1

629	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 2) contains the 2-affine cover
12	Mayer-Vietoris theorems, the cover-totality bridges (universe-shift
13	`LinearEquiv`s, `finrank` / `Module.Finite` transports), the

18	hypothesis.

20	This is cohort 2 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
21	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
22	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean` (this file): 2-affine cover MV +

29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u v w w'
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	section AffineCoverMVSquare
40	
41	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): a bundled 2-affine cover of a scheme
42	together with the affineness of the pairwise intersection. The geometric input
43	to the abstract Mayer-Vietoris LES `HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes — packaged
44	to record the affineness conditions and the cover-totality hypothesis that the
45	abstract MV-square does not capture. The accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`
46	produces the underlying Mathlib `MayerVietorisSquare`.
47	
48	Mathematically: a 2-affine open cover of `X` whose pairwise intersection is also
49	affine. The cover hypothesis `cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` is load-bearing for downstream
50	Serre-finiteness use (it identifies the `MayerVietorisSquare` corner `X₄` with
51	the whole scheme `X`). -/
52	structure AffineCoverMVSquare (X : Scheme.{u}) where
53	  /-- First affine open of the cover. -/
54	  U₁ : X.Opens
55	  /-- Second affine open of the cover. -/
56	  U₂ : X.Opens
57	  /-- `U₁` is affine. -/
58	  isAffineOpen_U₁ : IsAffineOpen U₁
59	  /-- `U₂` is affine. -/
60	  isAffineOpen_U₂ : IsAffineOpen U₂
61	  /-- The intersection `U₁ ⊓ U₂` is affine. -/
62	  isAffineOpen_inf : IsAffineOpen (U₁ ⊓ U₂)
63	  /-- Total cover: `U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤`. -/
64	  cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤
65	
66	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): the underlying abstract Mayer-Vietoris
67	square produced by a 2-affine cover. Bridge from the geometric input (an
68	`AffineCoverMVSquare`) to the categorical input (a `MayerVietorisSquare` for
69	the Grothendieck topology on `Opens X.toTopCat`) the abstract MV-LES theorem
70	`HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes. The four corners collapse definitionally:
71	`X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`, `X₂ = U₁`, `X₃ = U₂`, `X₄ = U₁ ⊔ U₂` (each by `rfl`,
72	plan-agent probe-verified). -/
73	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare {X : Scheme.{u}}
74	    (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
75	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat).MayerVietorisSquare :=
76	  Opens.mayerVietorisSquare S.U₁ S.U₂
77	
78	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`. The first three corners are
79	`rfl`-equal because `Opens.mayerVietorisSquare` defines them definitionally. -/
80	@[simp]
81	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₁
82	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
83	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₁ = S.U₁ ⊓ S.U₂ := rfl
84	
85	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₂ = U₁`. -/
86	@[simp]
87	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₂
88	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
89	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₂ = S.U₁ := rfl
90	
91	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₃ = U₂`. -/
92	@[simp]
93	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₃
94	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
95	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₃ = S.U₂ := rfl
96	
97	/-- Iter-029 cover-totality identification: `X₄ = ⊤`. The substantive corner
98	identification — uses the `cover` field, which is the totality hypothesis
99	`U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` recorded in `AffineCoverMVSquare`. -/
100	@[simp]
101	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄
102	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
103	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄ = ⊤ := S.cover
104	
105	/-- Iter-029 Mayer-Vietoris LES sequence on a 2-affine cover: a routine
106	specialisation of the iter-022 abstract sequence `HModule'_sequence` to the
107	bundled `AffineCoverMVSquare` data. Parameterised over a generic sheaf `F`
108	for reusability; the `toModuleKSheaf C` application is iter-030+ work. The
109	fully-qualified `_root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence` reference
110	is required because `def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` auto-opens the
111	`AffineCoverMVSquare` namespace inside the body, causing bare
112	`HModule'_sequence` to recursively resolve to itself. -/
113	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence
114	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
115	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
116	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
117	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
118	  _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence k S.toMayerVietorisSquare F n₀ n₁ h
119	
120	/-- Iter-029 Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness on a 2-affine cover: a routine
121	specialisation of the iter-026 abstract exactness theorem
122	`HModule'_sequence_exact` to the bundled `AffineCoverMVSquare` data. -/
123	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_exact
124	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
125	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
126	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
127	    (S.HModule'_sequence k F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
128	  _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence_exact k S.toMayerVietorisSquare F n₀ n₁ h
129	
130	/-- Iter-030 Mayer-Vietoris LES on a 2-affine cover of a curve: a routine
131	binding of the iter-029 sheaf-parameterised `HModule'_sequence` to the
132	structure sheaf `toModuleKSheaf C` for `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`.
133	The dot-notation `S.HModule'_sequence` resolves cleanly via method-call
134	against `S : AffineCoverMVSquare`, sidestepping the iter-029 sub-namespace
135	shadowing trap that required `_root_` disambiguation in the abstract
136	specialisation's body. -/
137	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_curve
138	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
139	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
140	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
141	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
142	  S.HModule'_sequence k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n₀ n₁ h
143	
144	/-- Iter-030 Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness on a 2-affine cover of a curve:
145	the exactness companion of `HModule'_sequence_curve`, by direct application
146	of the iter-029 abstract `HModule'_sequence_exact` to the structure sheaf
147	`toModuleKSheaf C`. -/
148	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_curve_exact
149	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
150	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
151	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
152	    (S.HModule'_sequence_curve k n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
153	  S.HModule'_sequence_exact k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n₀ n₁ h
154	
155	end AffineCoverMVSquare
156	
157	/-- Iter-031 cover-totality source-object identification. For a category `C`
158	with terminal object `T`, the sheafification of the free `k`-module on the
159	representable presheaf at `T` is canonically isomorphic to the constant sheaf
160	at `ModuleCat.of k k`. This is the natural-iso prerequisite for identifying
161	`HModule' k F n T` (open-evaluation cohomology at the terminal `T`, iter-014)
162	with `HModule k F n` (global cohomology, iter-009): both are `Ext` groups
163	whose source objects are made canonically isomorphic by this iso.
164	
165	Assembled from three Mathlib pieces, mirroring the structure
166	`constantSheaf J D = Functor.const Cᵒᵖ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J D`:
167	1. `yoneda.obj T ≅ (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit` — the terminal-collapse
168	   of the representable, by `IsTerminal.from`/`IsTerminal.hom_ext`.
169	2. `Functor.constComp _ PUnit (ModuleCat.free k)` — the identity
170	   `(Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit ⋙ ModuleCat.free k =
171	    (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj ((ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit)`
172	   packaged as an iso.
173	3. `(Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso` —
174	   `(ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit ≅ ModuleCat.of k k`. -/
175	noncomputable def HModule'_top_sourceIso
176	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
177	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
178	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
179	    {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
180	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj
181	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)
182	      ≅ (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) :=
183	  (presheafToSheaf J _).mapIso
184	    (Functor.isoWhiskerRight
185	        (NatIso.ofComponents
186	          (fun _ => Equiv.toIso { toFun := fun _ => PUnit.unit
187	                                  invFun := fun _ => hT.from _
188	                                  left_inv := fun _ => hT.hom_ext _ _
189	                                  right_inv := fun _ => rfl })
190	          (fun _ => by ext; rfl))
191	        (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
192	      Functor.constComp _ PUnit.{u+1} (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
193	      (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).mapIso
194	        (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso)
195	
196	/-- Iter-032 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u+1`. Combining the
197	iter-031 `HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source
198	objects of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
199	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element) yields
200	a `k`-linear equivalence between the universe-`u+1` Ext at the sheafified
201	representable on `T` and `HModule k F n`.
202	
203	The universe annotation `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}` on the LHS is load-bearing —
204	without it Lean would pick `Ext.{u}` (matching iter-014's `HModule'` choice),
205	and the equivalence would not typecheck against `HModule k F n : Type (u+1)`
206	(iter-009). By pinning the LHS to `Ext.{u+1}` we sidestep the iter-031
207	universe mismatch (`HModule' : Type u` vs `HModule : Type (u+1)`); both
208	sides of this equivalence live at `Type (u+1)`. The bridge from
209	`HModule' k F n T : Type u` to the LHS here is iter-033+ work — most
210	plausibly via a small refactor on iter-014 to align `HModule'`'s universe,
211	or via a `ULift.{u+1}` wrapper.
212	
213	Body uses `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two pre-composition maps via the

215	rewrite: associativity (with the middle factor degree 0), composition

217	noncomputable def HModule_top_linearEquiv
218	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
219	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
220	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
221	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
222	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
223	    Abelian.Ext.{u+1}
224	        ((presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
225	          ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)) F n
226	      ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n :=
227	  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
228	  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
229	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
230	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
231	    (by
232	      ext x
233	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
234	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
235	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
236	          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
237	    (by
238	      ext y
239	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
240	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
241	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
242	          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
243	
244	/-- Iter-033 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u`. The universe-`u`
245	parallel of iter-032's `HModule_top_linearEquiv`. Combining the iter-031
246	`HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source objects
247	of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
248	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element)
249	yields a `k`-linear equivalence between `HModule' k F n T` (the iter-014
250	universe-`u` cohomology of an open at the terminal) and the universe-`u`"}

## Target 1: `h_loc_exact` at L1783 (now L1802) — PARTIAL

The deferral is structurally honest: the geometric setup already in tactic-state is reusable by iter-109's continuation. |

### Lemmas verified via lean_run_code + lean_leansearch this iter

- **`LocalizedModule.map_exact`** — verified to exist in `Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Exact`, **CIRCULAR**: its hypothesis is `Function.Exact f_R g_R` (which is exactly `h_K₀_exact`, the lemma we are constructing). Confirmed via lean_run_code output `@LocalizedModule.map_exact`.

- **`LinearEquiv.conj_exact_iff_exact`** — verified one-iso variant available as backup.
- **`IsLocalizedModule.map_iso_commute`** — verified; commutes `LocalizedModule.map` with `IsLocalizedModule.iso` (will be needed for the recipe's commutation diagram).
- **`IsLocalizedModule.pi`** — verified via lean_run_code as the typeclass instance providing the finite-product LocalizedModule structure (recipe Step 2).
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`** — verified; the geometric "basic-open is a localization" lemma for affine schemes (recipe Step 1a).
- **`instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid`** — verified; the algebra adapter from `IsLocalization` (commutative-ring level) to `IsLocalizedModule` (module level).

```lean
        -- coordinate-identification bridging `∏ᶜ Z₂` to the slice-cover degree-`n` term.
        sorry
```

**`LocalizedModule.map_exact` direct application** — confirmed CIRCULAR via the analogist's Q1c PROCEED note and re-verified by the prover via `lean_run_code` on the lemma signature. ## Overall progress (this session detail)

- **Total active syntactic sorry sites**: **14**, distributed:
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`: **6** at L1120 (`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` — PAUSED iter-107 partial-proof scaffold preserved), L1212 (substep (a) augmented Čech, deferred), L1536 (`K → K₀` transport, deferred), L1564 (substep (a) for `s₀`, deferred), L1754 (`g_R.map_smul'`, gated on L1120 closure), **L1802 (former L1783 `h_loc_exact` — iter-108 partial scaffolding committed; deferred Steps 1c-4)**.

- **Pivot from L1120 to L1783 as the substantive lane**. Strategy-critic-iter106 named this pivot as a **critical alternative**; progress-critic-iter106 STUCK verdict on L1120 made staying on the slot equivalent to ratifying the planner's own prior failure pattern. - `IsLocalizedModule.pi` is the canonical Mathlib idiom for finite-product localisation.

## Next iter

Iter-109 (Archon iter-107) substantive lane: close L1802 (Steps 1c-4 of analogist Q1 recipe, ~100-120 LOC inline). C1 promotion remains queued. L1120 remains PAUSED.

Both proofs are internally consistent and the prerequisite helpers (`SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`, `Derivation.postcomp_comp`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta_hη`) are honestly closed.

212	## Must-fix-this-iter
213	
214	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean:85` — `def LineBundle (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := CommRing.Pic (X.presheaf.obj (op (⊤ : X.Opens)))`. **Weakened-wrong definition** standing in for "invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module". The file docstring itself documents the mismatch (strict subgroup on non-affine `X`; gets `Pic ℙ¹ = trivial` whereas the true `Pic ℙ¹ = ℤ`). Why must-fix: per directive item (1) and the must-fix verbatim rule, weakened-wrong definitions land here regardless of how thoroughly they are documented as "stand-ins" — the documentation makes the wrongness explicit, it does not absolve it. Severity: **critical** (carried forward from iter-105).
215	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean:166-173` — `instIsMonoidal_W : (W X).IsMonoidal := by … sorry`. **Sorry-bodied typeclass instance on a substantive claim.** Why must-fix: a `sorry`-bodied `instance` is a typeclass-search-visible declaration; even if no current consumer dispatches on it, it is registered for synthesis and propagates the sorry into any future code that asks for `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` via the localization route. The defence "does NOT block downstream consumers" (L161–164) is a deferral excuse, not a correctness argument. Severity: **critical** (carried forward from iter-105).
216	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean:190` — `PicardFunctor.representable := sorry` on a load-bearing existence claim. The file docstring (L26–36) explicitly states keeping it as sorry "to avoid asserting representability of the wrong functor". Why must-fix: the rationale is correct but it documents a *downstream* symptom of the LineBundle weakened-wrong def; the sorry will only become honestly closeable once `LineBundle` is fixed. Counted at must-fix to keep the dependency on the LineBundle fix visible — once LineBundle is the invertible-quasi-coherent-sheaf object, this representability can either close (FGA / Mathlib) or remain as a clearly-labelled Phase-C deferral whose obstruction is no longer the wrong def. Severity: **major** (downstream of the critical LineBundle issue, not independently load-bearing).
217	
218	## Major
219	
220	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean:39-61` — **Dead-code sketch block** preserved as a multi-line `/- … -/` comment. Reproduces an old proof outline (`OXAsAddCommGrpSheaf`, `H1OC`) that has been entirely replaced by the iter-009 `HModule` route. Stale; should be deleted or replaced with a one-line note pointing at the current body. Severity: major (genuinely outdated comment, not just stylistic noise).
221	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:1212` — `h_a := sorry` (substep (a), extra-degeneracy on slice cover). Substantive, not WIP-exempt per the directive (only L1120 and L1802 are exempt). Severity: major (scaffolded, mathematically defensible, but open).
222	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:1536` — `h_transport := sorry` (Čech-cohomology refinement transport). Substantive. Same severity.
223	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:1564` — `h_a₀ := sorry` (substep (a) on `s₀`-indexed cover). Substantive. Same severity.
224	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:1754` — `g_R.map_smul' := sorry`. The mirror of `f_R.map_smul'` (closed). The comment at L1744–1753 is concrete about the blocker (`Eq.mpr`-transport through `CochainComplex.next`); resolvable next iter. Severity: major.
225	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:122` — `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf := sorry`. The Phase B step 1 main gap. Comment at L98–112 lays out the proof outline (Substep 1 `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule`, Substep 2 basis sheaf condition, Substep 3 globalisation). Honest scaffolding sorry, not flagged as critical because Phase B is acknowledged as a multi-iteration project, but it is still load-bearing for downstream Differentials consumers (`relativeDifferentials` at L128 and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` exactness at L636). Severity: major.
226	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:636` — `cotangentExactSeq_structure.h_exact := sorry`. Documented as requiring `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` (multi-iteration). Severity: major.
227	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:718, 735, 877` — three more Phase B step-2 sorries (`smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, `cotangent_at_section`, `serre_duality_genus`). Phase B headline obligations. Severity: major.
228	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:179` — `nonempty_jacobianWitness := sorry`. The single Phase C existence-of-Albanese sorry, packaging the four downstream `Jacobian C` instances. Major (it is the single project sorry that the entire Phase E reduction depends on; the L162–175 docstring documents the mathematical content honestly).
229	
230	## Minor
231	
232	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean:111-112` — universe annotation `AddCommGrpCat.{max u (u+1)}` is needlessly verbose; `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` is equivalent (since `max u (u+1) = u+1`). Optional readability cleanup.
233	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean:458-466` (`IsAffineHModuleHomFinite`) and L476–488 (`module_finite_HModule'_zero_of_isAffineHModuleHomFinite`) — the class itself is documented at L527–542 as "dead scaffolding" (incompatible with non-trivial proper curves: `Γ(U, O_C)` is not k-finite on affine opens of `ℙ¹`). The pivot to `IsHModuleHomFinite` (iter-043, L544–549) is correct, but the dead class and its consumer remain in the file. Removing them would tighten the API.
234	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — heartbeat ladder (`maxHeartbeats 800000` at L1128, `1600000` at L908) is high. Once L1120 closes, revisit budget.
235	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean:120-159` — the in-docstring "Iter-080 investigation (stalks-level route)" block embeds the prover's diagnostic narrative. Helpful for continuity but tightens the file. Optional refactor into `analogies/` or `.archon/logs/`.
236	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietorisCore.lean:343-348` (`ModuleCat_free_preservesMonomorphisms`) — fine as-is, but a one-line PR to upstream Mathlib's `ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean` would remove this project-local instance entirely. Track as upstream candidate.
237	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean:1622-1700` — the `letI perI₁`, `letI h_mod_pi₁`, ... block is reconstructed body-locally in `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` (L547–566), `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear` (L689–708), and `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` itself (L972–991). Could be extracted to a single helper definition. Not breaking, but noisy.
238	
239	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
240	
241	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean:18-60, 71-84`: extensive "*first-approximation*" / "*non-vacuous stand-in*" / "*strict subgroup in general*" framing around the L85–86 weakened-wrong `def LineBundle`. The docstring is intellectually honest about the mismatch, but it is precisely the kind of declaration the lean-auditor flags as a critical excuse-comment: an admission, attached to a load-bearing definition, that the definition is wrong on non-affine `X`. Severity: **critical**.
242	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean:161-165`: "this sorry does NOT block downstream consumers, … Phase C step C1 (LineBundle refinement) is unblocked." This is a deferral excuse attached to a load-bearing sorry-bodied `instance`. Severity: **critical**.
243	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean:26-36`: "intentionally left as `sorry` — … forbidden shortcut: keep it as `sorry`." Mathematically the rationale is correct (closing on the weakened-LineBundle `PicardFunctor` would assert representability of the wrong functor). But the comment frames a sorry-on-load-bearing-claim as a workflow rule, which the lean-auditor lint identifies as an excuse-comment. Severity: **major** (downstream of LineBundle).
244	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:27-31`: "**Status (iteration 064 — scaffold)** All main declarations have `sorry` bodies. Closure trajectory is estimated at ~10 iterations." Multi-sorry status block. Each individual sorry is mathematically defensible but the *aggregate* framing — "this whole file is scaffold for ten more iterations" — is the kind of comment that normalises sorries as workflow. Severity: **major**.
245	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:162-175` (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` docstring): "the existence is assumed and the proof is deferred to a future iteration." Severity: **major** (single high-impact deferral, plan-bless­ed by strategy).
246	
247	## Severity summary
248	
249	- **must-fix-this-iter**: 3 — `LineBundle` weakened-wrong def (critical), `instIsMonoidal_W := sorry` (critical), `PicardFunctor.representable := sorry` (major, downstream of LineBundle).
250	- **major**: 11 — `Genus.lean` dead-code sketch; the four non-exempt BasicOpenCech sorries (L1212, L1536, L1564, L1754); five Differentials sorries (L122, L636, L718, L735, L877); `Jacobian.lean:179` (`nonempty_jacobianWitness`).
251	- **minor**: 6 — universe-annotation simplification (`FunctorAb.lean`); dead `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` class (`StructureSheafModuleK.lean`); BasicOpenCech heartbeat ladder; in-docstring diagnostic narrative (`Modules/Monoidal.lean`); upstream-Mathlib candidate (`ModuleCat_free_preservesMonomorphisms`); repeated local `letI` blocks in `BasicOpenCech.lean`.
252	- **excuse-comments**: 5 (also counted under must-fix-this-iter / major above; the LineBundle one and the `instIsMonoidal_W` one alone are critical; the others are major).
253	
254	Overall verdict: two long-standing critical structural defects (the weakened-wrong `LineBundle` and `instIsMonoidal_W := sorry`) remain exactly as flagged in iter-105 and continue to dictate downstream deferrals (`PicardFunctor.representable`); the iter-108-cleanup refactor of the `## Status` blocks in `StructureSheafModuleK.lean` and `Rigidity.lean` is accurate and the directive-exempt iter-106 prover work in `BasicOpenCech.lean` (L1120 `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` and L1802 `h_loc_exact`) is mathematically defensible plan-blessed WIP.
255

## Knowledge Base

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **2-line geometric setup for localised slice-cover identification**
  *(NEW iter-108)*: when proving exactness of a localised differential
  at a coord `f ∈ s₀` inside an `exact_of_isLocalized_span` outer
  scaffold (the `h_K₀_exact` shape at `BasicOpenCech.lean:1781-1802`),
  the boilerplate first two lemmas are:
  (i) `have h_V_le_U (x) : (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (x a)) ≤ U
      := by refine (Pi.π _ ⟨0, by omega⟩).le.trans ?_; exact basicOpen_le _ _`,
  (ii) `have h_slice_eq (x) : (∏ᶜ ...) ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 =
      C.left.basicOpen ((presheaf.map (homOfLE (h_V_le_U x)).op).hom f.1)
      := (Scheme.basicOpen_res C.left f.1 _).symm`.
  These are one-liners modulo coercion bookkeeping and should be the
  first two `have`s of any future Mathlib-aligned localised-Čech proof.
  **Documented at L1786-L1795 in BasicOpenCech.lean.**
- **`IsLocalizedModule.pi` is the canonical Mathlib idiom for
  finite-product localisation** *(NEW iter-108 — RECONFIRMS analogist
  Q1a)*: the typeclass instance at
  `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi:93` provides
  `IsLocalizedModule S (LinearMap.pi fun i ↦ f i ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj i)`
  for finite `ι`. Always consume via `IsLocalizedModule.iso` + the
  Mathlib instance; never re-derive the product-localisation
  commutation. Bridge to `Limits.Pi.lift`-form via `ModuleCat.piIsoPi`.
  **Persistent rationale at
  `analogies/finite-product-localisation-and-cech-r-linearity.md`.**
- **`Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` is the closing tool for
  localised-exactness transport (not `LocalizedModule.map_exact`)**
  *(NEW iter-108 — anti-pattern + correct route)*: in an
  `exact_of_isLocalized_span` outer scaffold the localised exactness
  goal is `Function.Exact ⇑(LocalizedModule.map S f_R)
  ⇑(LocalizedModule.map S g_R)`. The Mathlib lemma
  `LocalizedModule.map_exact` would close this from `Function.Exact f_R g_R`,
  but that is exactly the `h_K₀_exact` we are constructing — **circular**.
  Correct route: use `IsLocalizedModule.iso` (from `IsLocalizedModule.pi`)
  to transport slice-cover function-level exactness (already in scope
  as `h_a₀_fun f`) across a `LinearEquiv` ladder via
  `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` (or the one-iso variant
  `LinearEquiv.conj_exact_iff_exact`). **Documented at iter-108
  `task_results/Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.lean.md` + the analogies file.**
- **Body-local `have h_iter104 := <iter-104 R-linearity lemma> hU s₀ n hn i r' y'`
  as Route B staging** *(iter-107)*: at the post-S5 LinearMap-chain frame

### Known Blockers (do not retry)

- **`LocalizedModule.map_exact` direct application in
  `exact_of_isLocalized_span` outer scaffolds** *(NEW iter-108,
  anti-pattern)*: the lemma takes `Function.Exact f_R g_R` and
  produces `Function.Exact (LocalizedModule.map S f_R)
  (LocalizedModule.map S g_R)`. In the `h_K₀_exact` scaffold the

  tool inside an `exact_of_isLocalized_span` outer scaffold.** Use
  `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` + `IsLocalizedModule.pi`
  + `IsLocalizedModule.iso` instead (see Proof Patterns above).
- **Reuse of `h_loc_X_i f` directly without slice-cover bridge for
  `h_loc_exact`** *(NEW iter-108)*: `h_loc_X_i f` is
  `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1 (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap ...)` which
  is `localizedModuleIsLocalizedModule _` — trivial; contributes no
  slice-cover identification. The slice-cover bridge requires
  geometric `h_V_le_U` + `h_slice_eq` + the algebra adapter chain.

# For the user

Two long-standing strategic items still require user attention (carried from iter-104; lean-auditor-iter106 re-flagged at **critical**, unchanged severity):

- **`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundle.lean:85` — `def LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` is weakened-wrong on non-affine schemes.** Documented in the file (the docstring at L18-60 acknowledges the mismatch — strict subgroup in general; e.g. `Pic ℙⁿ_k = ℤ` while the current def returns trivial).
